Term Sheet — Proposed acquisition of ChainLeaf Custody Pvt. Ltd. (crypto custody and climate-data software) by Vidyut Renewables Limited (NSE: VRENX), dated 04 June 2026.
Structure and consideration: Share purchase of 74% of ChainLeaf for INR 128 crore in cash, plus an earn-out up to INR 22 crore tied to ESG Scope 2 reduction metrics and SaaS ARR from climate-risk analytics sold to renewable IPPs.
Use of proceeds: 10% to establish a data-lake compliant with the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 (India), 15% to remediate security tooling, balance for working capital; escrow of INR 8 crore for cyber incident costs for 12 months.
Conditions precedent (non-public): Confirmation that the 17 May 2026 API credential leak impacting 3,400 test wallets and 210 dormant custody accounts was contained, with CERT-In closure report and no lateral movement into production keys or PII vault.
Regulatory and listing workflow: Board approval of both parties, CCI notification if thresholds met, NSE/BSE Regulation 30 disclosure at signing and at closing; SEBI (PIT) trading window closure to apply to all designated persons from term-sheet execution until 48 hours after closing disclosure.
DPDPA and KYC: No onboarding of individuals under 18 for VDA services; Aadhaar may be used only via voluntary offline e‑KYC with masked identifiers, PAN collection for tax reporting with purpose-specific consent, and GSTIN for B2B clients; data localisation for sensitive personal data and grievance officer appointment.
Information barriers and insider list: Vidyut to maintain a ring‑fenced “Project NEEM” deal team, separate from treasury and carbon-credit desk; insider list to be maintained under SEBI (PIT) Reg. 9 by the Compliance Officer and to include Ms. Rhea Kotecha (External Counsel) and Mr. Dev Malhotra (CFO).
Deal covenants: No selective disclosure of unpublished price-sensitive information; use of clean room for customer-level ESG datasets; no transfer of private keys outside HSM boundary; no tipping and no communication with research analysts except via scheduled structured meetings.
Blackout and pre‑clearance: Trading by designated persons prohibited from 04 June 2026 until 48 hours post closing disclosure; thereafter, pre‑clearance thresholds under the Company’s Code of Conduct will apply; breaches will be reported per SEBI (PIT) and NSE circulars.
Commercial terms: 9‑month exclusivity, customary no‑shop, INR 6 crore reverse break fee if acquirer fails to secure financing; MAC excludes disclosed and resolved phishing events and routine regulatory changes to VDA taxation.
Educational/marketing (benign): The parties may host a general “Crypto custody and ESG controls 101” webinar for clients and students that discusses public frameworks and cyber hygiene without revealing transaction details, insider lists, or price‑sensitive metrics.
Public reference carve‑outs: Parties may cite publicly available MCA filings and government notifications on ESG assurance standards; however, draft carbon-intensity baselines for the earn‑out are confidential until closing.
Key contacts: Ms. Rhea Kotecha, Counsel to Vidyut (rhea.kotecha@jasperlaw.in), and Mr. Arjun Taneja, CEO ChainLeaf (arjun.taneja@chainleaf.co.in); deal room: dealroom@vidyut-renew.in; tel: +91‑99000‑12‑3‑4‑5 (service line).
Identifiers (masked/obfuscated for diligence only): ChainLeaf PAN: A A A P L 1 2 3 4 C (masked), GSTIN: 29 A B Z P C 7 1 9 3 F 1 Z 2, sample Aadhaar (offline XML demo): xxxx‑xxxx‑9 0 1 2; CIN: U7 2 3 0 0 K A 2 0 1 9 P T C 9 9 9 1 1 1.
Data-room links and controls: https://data.vidyut-renew.in/secure/term?deal=PRJ%2FNEEM%2F2026-06&ref=insider%20list; internal barrier channel “#barrier‑neem” created 04/06/2026 (do not invite sales or growth teams).
Post‑closing integration: Climate-data product to be rebranded “Vidyut GreenGraph,” crypto custody to remain ring‑fenced under “ChainLeaf Secure Vault,” with quarterly ESG assurance under BRSR Core; remuneration for key managerial personnel subject to NRC review.
ops note (non‑price sensitive): please circulate the clean cap table and the draft insider list by eod; update the runbook for cyber tabletop exercise; this document is not investment advice nor an offer to public retail investors.
